div.head: The Minister in China (Johnson) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 933. I heard late last night, and had it confirmed this morning, that the Japanese have officially demanded that all Chinese troops be withdrawn 20 li, namely about 6⅔ miles, from Tientsin under provisional note of July 15, 1902 (see MacMurray’s Treaties, volume I, page 317).Note of July 15, 1902, also printed in Foreign Relations, 1902, p. 198. Donald states has no knowledge Young Marshal has promised to comply but will increase Tientsin police force. YoungC. Walter Young, an American citizen and writer. claims only about two regiments of Chinese troops were within radius mentioned.
Repeated Tientsin, Nanking and Commander in Chief.
